title: Hotel Front Desk Agent

rationales.taskAutomation: Check-in, check-out, and basic reservation and FAQ handling are highly standardized information tasks that mobile check-in and kiosks already perform end-to-end.
rationales.toolMaturity: Mobile check-in, keyless entry, and AI chatbot concierge tools are mature and deployed across major hotel brands.
rationales.adoption: Mobile check-in and self-service kiosks are now mainstream at mid-size and large chains, with adoption still spreading to independents.
rationales.agenticExposure: Reservation lookup, room assignment, and check-in/out confirmation form a complete agent-traversable booking pipeline.

tasksAtRisk: Basic reservation changes and FAQ responses
tasksGrowing: Local recommendations tailored to individual guests
Handling groups, VIPs, and non-standard requests
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-4081, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Hotel PMS and AI check-in system administration
Cross-training toward guest-relations or revenue roles

insight: Mobile check-in and keyless entry already handle the routine arrival for most guests. The desk agent's value has shifted to the guests self-service can't handle: the VIP request, the overbooking problem, the complaint that needs a human decision.
